task_completion.description: The original nose cone is replaced with a transition, a wider payload bay body tube, and a new nose cone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenRocket GUI, the rocket design view or component tree shows a transition, a wider body tube (payload bay), and a new nose cone at the front of the rocket.

task_completion.description: A 50g mass component is added to the payload bay.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket component tree shows a 'Mass component' inside the new payload bay body tube. If a configuration window is visible, it should show a mass of approximately 50g.

task_completion.description: The modified rocket is stabilized to achieve a stability margin of at least 1.0 caliber.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The stability margin indicator in the top right of the OpenRocket design window shows a value of 1.00 cal or greater.

task_completion.description: A flight simulation is run using an appropriate motor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab in OpenRocket shows a completed simulation run with a motor (e.g., C6-3 or C6-5) and displays results such as apogee.

task_completion.description: The modified design is saved to '~/Documents/rockets/payload_lofter.ork'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket window title shows 'payload_lofter.ork', or a file manager/terminal shows this file exists in the '~/Documents/rockets/' directory.

task_completion.description: A summary report is written documenting the final stability margin, the motor used, and the predicted apogee altitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the contents of 'expansion_report.txt' in '~/Documents/exports/', containing the stability margin, the motor name, and the apogee altitude.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the design and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenRocket application open with the agent interacting with its menus, component dialogs, or simulation tabs.

integrity.description: The values in the summary report match the actual simulation results from OpenRocket.
integrity.visual_evidence: The stability margin and apogee values written in the text report match the numbers visible in the OpenRocket GUI screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by directly writing or downloading the final .ork XML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a progression of screenshots showing the rocket being built/modified step-by-step in the OpenRocket GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The modified rocket design must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/payload_lofter.ork'. The summary report must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/expansion_report.txt'. The passing threshold for this task is 65 points.
